data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cf79f10c">
    <title>fix(escalation): no_changes smart retry climbs ladder one step at a time</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:61-76
  investigationRetryInitialMinPower jumps DIRECTLY to the highest
  viable MinPower when applyNoChangesSmartRetry sets
  Extra["triage.tier_hint"]==TierSmart. This is a binary jump
  (current power → top of ladder), violating ADR-024 P2 ("escalate
  on failure" — implies stepwise climb).

  Net effect: a no-changes outcome on a cheap-tier model leaps
  immediately to top tier on the next retry. If standard tier would
  have succeeded, the user pays top-tier cost unnecessarily.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2712-2735
    applyNoChangesSmartRetry: writes Extra["triage.tier_hint"]=TierSmart.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:61-76
    investigationRetryInitialMinPower: when hint==TierSmart, returns
    the highest viable MinPower from the ladder.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go applyNoChangesSmartRetry:
    write the NEXT-tier MinPower floor (current actualPower + 1
    ladder step) into Extra["triage.tier_hint"], NOT TierSmart as a
    name.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go
    investigationRetryInitialMinPower: read the numeric floor from
    Extra and return that, not the top of ladder.
  - When the bead has been through cheap → standard already and
    that still produced no_changes, write the standard+1 step
    (smart). When standard → smart still no_changes, status=proposed
    (genuine spec gap; operator must clarify).

  Tests:
     - TestNoChangesSmartRetry_StepwiseClimb: assert each no_changes
       failure bumps the next-attempt MinPower by exactly one ladder
       step.
     - TestNoChangesSmartRetry_TopTierExhausted_GoesToProposed:
       assert when no_changes happens at the top tier, the bead is
       parked to status=proposed (genuine spec gap, not retry).

NON-SCOPE
  - General ladder mechanics (cli/internal/agent/escalation/).
  - Refactoring the smart-retry hint format.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P2 principle.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. applyNoChangesSmartRetry writes the next-tier MinPower (current+1 ladder step) into Extra, not the TierSmart name.
2. investigationRetryInitialMinPower returns the recorded next-tier floor, not the top of the ladder.
3. TestNoChangesSmartRetry_StepwiseClimb in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go asserts each no_changes bumps MinPower by exactly one step.
4. TestNoChangesSmartRetry_TopTierExhausted_GoesToProposed asserts top-tier no_changes parks to status=proposed.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, spec:ADR-024</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="4be11ccddd1696e468a3b176cdd6ce4887bdc46b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T205459-2a6794a1"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed at `9c141c4bb`. All 6 ACs are satisfied:

1. **AC1**: `applyNoChangesSmartRetry` now writes `nextFloor` (int from `EscalationNextFloor(actualPower)`) into `Extra[TriageTierHintKey]` instead of the string `"smart"`.
2. **AC2**: `investigationRetryInitialMinPower` checks `numericTierFloorHint` first and returns the stored floor directly when found, without calling `highestViableEscalationFloor`.
3. **AC3**: `TestNoChangesSmartRetry_StepwiseClimb` — two successive no_changes at powers 50 and 70 produce hints 70 and 90 respectively (one step each).
4. **AC4**: `TestNoChangesSmartRetry_TopTierExhausted_GoesToProposed` — exhausted ladder parks bead to `status=proposed`.
5. **AC5**: `go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` green.
6. **AC6**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.
